channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: James Silk Buckingham: Journalist, Reformer, and Advocate for Press Freedom in Colonial India
item.description: In 1818, James Silk Buckingham arrived in Calcutta and soon faced a moral crisis when asked to transport enslaved people to Zanzibar. Standing firmly against slavery, he refused, which left him strand...

item.title: Abandoned townhouse in Barrie catches fire late at night
item.description: A fire broke out late last night at an abandoned townhouse in Barrie, Ontario. Emergency services responded quickly to the scene, ensuring that the fire did not spread to neighbouring properties. Fire...

item.title: New Zealand’s Remarkable Journey to the T20 World Cup Final
item.description: The article reflects on New Zealand’s unexpected journey to the T20 World Cup final, portraying it less as a sporting achievement and more as a reflection of a distinctive mindset. The author draws pa...

item.title: Alex Eala reaches third round in Indian Wells Open, to face Coco Gauff again
item.description: Alex Eala showcased her resilience in her debut at the 2026 Indian Wells Open, narrowly defeating Ukraine's Dayana Yastremska 7-5, 4-6, 7-5 in the round of 64. Despite facing a 4-5 deficit in the deci...

item.title: How long-term and working memory explain why old song lyrics are easy to recall but recent intentions are often forgotten
item.description: The article explores a common experience: effortlessly recalling every lyric of a song from 25 years ago, even rapping along, whilst immediately forgetting the reason for entering a room. This contras...

item.title: Federal High Court directs INEC to recognise Dr Oluwole Oluyede as PDP governorship candidate for Ekiti 2026 election
item.description: For the Ekiti State governorship election slated for June 2026, a Federal High Court sitting in Abuja has given a landmark ruling in favour of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) and its candidate, Dr ...

item.title: Bahati’s New Track Fails to Impress Fans Amid Music Feud
item.description: Kenyan musician Bahati recently dropped a diss track titled 'Lost Kwa Throne', targeting fellow artistes Toxic Lyrikali and Buruklyn Boyz. The song, which premiered on YouTube on March 6, 2026, aimed ...

item.title: President Tinubu Promises Better Welfare for Soldiers and Confirms Nigeria's Economy Has Stabilised
item.description: President Bola Tinubu, during an interfaith breaking of fast event in Abuja with members of the Armed Forces and senior government officials, assured Nigerian troops that his administration will prior...

item.title: Madras High Court Highlights Impact of Pocso on Boys in Adolescent Relationships
item.description: The Madras High Court, through Justice N Mala of the Madurai bench, recently set aside the conviction of a 17-year-old boy under the Protection of Children from Sexual Offences (Pocso) Act. The case i...

item.title: US Corporate Media's Selective Empathy for Iranians in Coverage of Strikes on Iran
item.description: On February 28, 2026, the United States and Israel launched joint military strikes on Iran, killing Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei and targeting nuclear, missile, and security sites, in what wa...

item.title: Kristi Noem Fired as DHS Secretary Amid Controversy Over Spending and Internal Management
item.description: Kristi Noem, who served as Secretary of Homeland Security, was recently fired by President Trump, though the reasons behind her dismissal were more political than operational. Noem focused heavily on ...

item.title: Conflict in Iran escalates with regional attacks as diplomatic efforts start
item.description: The war in Iran don enter week two as US President Donald Trump dey insist say e go accept only Tehran's 'unconditional surrender'. This come as Israel dey continue military operations both inside Ira...

item.title: Pakistan Balances Support to Saudi Arabia with Diplomatic Caution Towards Iran
item.description: Pakistan is walking a careful diplomatic line as tensions in the Gulf rise following Iranian drone and missile strikes on Saudi territory. The recent military consultations between Islamabad and Riyad...

item.title: Philippine Coast Guard identifies missing captain from sunken M/V Trisha Kerstin 3
item.description: Police forensic specialists have confirmed that the missing captain of M/V Trisha Kerstin 3, Capt. Sonie Secuban, was among the recovered bodies from the sunken vessel off Baluk-Baluk Island, Basilan....

item.title: Affordable Commuter Towns in Great Britain for 2026
item.description: The commuter belt in Great Britain is evolving as homebuyers search for affordability and convenience. Following the pandemic, many moved away from traditional commuter areas seeking more space and ru...

item.title: Remembering Rev Jesse Jackson: Champion of Civil Rights and Ally of South Africa
item.description: The Nelson Mandela Foundation recently held a memorial tribute to honour Reverend Jesse Jackson, reflecting on his global impact as a civil rights leader and his longstanding solidarity with South Afr...

item.title: Alberta to Lead Environmental Assessments for Major Projects Under New Federal-Provincial Agreement
item.description: The federal government and Alberta have reached an agreement granting the province primary authority over regulatory approvals for major projects, aiming to streamline and accelerate project construct...

item.title: Bandits Attempt Kidnap in Eruku, Kwara; Local Security Repels Attack
item.description: Early Friday morning, residents of Eruku community for Ekiti Local Government Area, Kwara State, were shaken as heavily armed bandits stormed their village around 5 a.m. According to locals, the attac...

item.title: Shaina Nitura leads Adamson University to clean sweep over UST in UAAP volleyball
item.description: In a dominant UAAP Season 88 volleyball match, Shaina Nitura showcased exceptional skills, leading the Adamson Lady Falcons to a straight-set victory against the UST Golden Tigresses. Nitura's perform...

item.title: Seven People Including Child Killed, Eight Injured in Road Collision on Makran Coastal Highway Due to Abandoned Gas Tanker
item.description: In a tragic road accident at Manjhi on the Makran Coastal Highway in Balochistan, at least seven people, including a young child, lost their lives while eight others sustained injuries. According to p...
